metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact predicate for 'requires a graph/log' (e.g. a new `requires_graph` flag on the criterion descriptor, or an explicit id set) and co-locate it with the criterion arm dispatch rule.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that criteria 'whose fixtures require a graph/log (Container G3/G4 + ISF) error with a clear message' but does not define the decision rule for detecting this condition — specifically, whether detection is based on `exec_tier == AGENT`, a hard-coded id set, or something else. ISF has special handling in production_batch_runner (filtered by `cid == 'ISF'`), while G3/G4 are AGENT-tier but so are T1, T8, T10, T11, G6, etc., which may not require a graph.
findings.location: Invocation model section: 'Criterion arm of run_case' — 'Container G3/G4 + ISF need a graph/log, not inline text — OUT of scope here; a criterion whose fixtures require them errors with a clear message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` to the scope file list and specify the `criteria` subcommand intercept arm in `main()` (mirroring the existing `prompt`, `llm`, `workflow` intercepts) plus registration in `_help.known_subcommands()`.
findings.finding: The plan's scope file list omits `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py`, which must be modified to add the `criteria` intercept arm in `main()` and register `criteria` in `_help.known_subcommands()`. Without this wiring, `rebar criteria eval <id>` is unreachable regardless of the implementation in `_llm_commands.py` — the dispatcher will hit the 'unknown subcommand' error path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope / Files section: `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` (add `if argv and argv[0] == 'criteria': return _criteria(argv[1:])` intercept in `main()`, import `_criteria` from `_llm_commands`, register `'criteria'` in `_help.known_subcommands()`). This is a one-liner intercept mirroring the existing `prompt` arm.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit guard in the criterion arm of `run_case` that detects G3/G4 (container_only) and ISF criteria and raises a `ValueError` with a clear message before calling `pass1_chunk`, rather than relying on downstream failure to surface the misrouting.
findings.finding: The criterion arm silently misroutes G3/G4 and ISF-tier criteria through `pass1_chunk` without a specified guard, rather than raising the 'clear message' the plan promises.
findings.location: Invocation model & implementation contract — Criterion arm of `run_case`
findings.suggested_fix: At the start of the criterion arm, after resolving `desc`, check: `if desc.get('applies_at', {}).get('container_only') or desc['id'] == 'ISF': raise ValueError(f"criterion {cid!r} requires container/graph context and cannot be run via the inline eval arm")`. This maps exactly onto the plan's stated intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify why per-case majority-vote + stability (calibrate_criterion) is the right aggregation model rather than reusing `run_eval`'s existing at_least(k)-across-epochs loop; if both are intentional, document the semantic distinction explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new per-case majority-vote + stability aggregation model in `calibrate_criterion` that is structurally different from the existing `at_least(k)`-across-epochs model in `run_eval`, with no justification for why a parallel aggregation pattern is warranted over reusing/extending `run_eval`'s epoch loop.
findings.location: Invocation model section — 'N-run stability (clears E2/T5e/G6 — N is configurable)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `calibrate_criterion` delegates to `eval.cohens_kappa` and `parity._recall_false_accept` (or their public equivalents) rather than re-implementing recall/false-accept/kappa; name the exact import paths in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan says it will 'reuse discrimination scorers, κ, parity' but does not specify which existing call sites `calibrate_criterion` delegates to for recall/false-accept/kappa, leaving open whether these metrics are re-implemented or delegated — `parity.py` has `_recall_false_accept` and `eval.py` has `cohens_kappa` as the existing prior art.
findings.location: Scope section ('reuse discrimination scorers, κ, parity') and 'Calibration metrics' subsection
findings.suggested_fix: In the `calibrate_criterion` spec, replace the inline metric formulas with explicit delegation: `from rebar.llm.eval import cohens_kappa` and expose `_recall_false_accept` from `parity.py` as a public function (or inline its logic once, citing the source).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly that `calibrate_criterion` reuses `eval_scorers._fired` (not a re-implemented formula), and reconcile the plan's prose definition with the existing function's `verdict`-key branch.
findings.finding: The plan's criterion-arm `_fired` definition (`len(verdict.get('findings') or []) > 0`) is stated in prose without specifying that it should reuse or diverge from the existing `eval_scorers._fired`, which has a different branch: when `'verdict'` is present in the output dict it checks for FAIL before looking at findings. The calibrate_criterion spec references this predicate but does not co-locate the decision rule about which `_fired` to call.
findings.location: Invocation model section: 'Verdict ↔ expect vocabulary reconciliation'; calibrate_criterion spec
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the inline formula with: '`_fired(out)` is the EXISTING `eval_scorers._fired` (no-re-impl): fires if `verdict == FAIL` when a verdict key is present, else if findings is non-empty.' Add a note that criterion-arm outputs never carry a `verdict` key so the findings branch always applies, but reuse the shared helper to stay coherent with the existing scorers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3 (or the remediation ACs) to explicitly require the calibration view to render per-criterion min/mean stability, so the CLI output is fully verifiable against a stated criterion.
findings.finding: The calibration view's `stability` output (min/mean stability printed in the CLI view) is described in the implementation body but is absent from the original AC3, which lists only four metrics (recall, false-accept, Cohen's κ, agreement %). The remediation AC2 covers `calibrate_criterion` computing stability, but no AC explicitly mandates the CLI *view* renders min/mean stability — leaving the rendered output partially uncovered.
findings.location: Original AC3 ('The calibration view prints recall, false-accept, Cohen's κ, agreement % per criterion') vs. 'N-run stability' section ('The view prints the min/mean stability so a flaky criterion is visible')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend original AC3 to: 'The calibration view prints recall, false-accept, Cohen's κ, agreement %, and min/mean N-run stability per criterion, with each metric defined; the eval verdict vocabulary is reconciled with the fixture expect.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (with a test) asserting that `run_case` returns a clear, non-traceback error when the criterion requires a graph/log execution context (e.g., Container/ISF tier), so the supported-criterion-type boundary is verifiable.
findings.finding: The plan's implementation contract explicitly restricts the criterion arm to Pass-1 / inline-text criteria and states that criteria requiring a graph/log (Container G3/G4 + ISF) error with a clear message — but this error path and the supported criterion subset are not captured in any Acceptance Criterion, leaving it unverifiable at review time.
findings.location: Implementation contract section: 'Container G3/G4 + ISF need a graph/log, not inline text — OUT of scope here; a criterion whose fixtures require them errors with a clear message.'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the remediation ACs: '`run_case` raises a clear `ValueError` (not a traceback) when the matched criterion's exec_tier requires graph/log context; a unit test with a fake AGENT/ISF criterion descriptor asserts the error message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deterministic test for `calibrate_criterion` (or its underlying N-run loop) with `runs≥2` and mixed fire/no-fire outcomes per case, asserting correct majority-vote verdict and non-trivial `stability` values (e.g., 0.67 for 2-of-3 agreement); explicitly document tie-breaking behavior for even N.
findings.finding: The N-run majority-vote and stability computation are new user-facing logic but the plan only specifies tests at N=1 (where stability is trivially 1.0 and majority is trivial); no test is specified for N>1 with mixed results (e.g., 2-of-3 agree, or N=2 with a 1-1 split), leaving the actual majority-vote and tie-breaking logic untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions) / 'calibrate_criterion computes ... stability with an injectable solve' + N-run stability section
findings.suggested_fix: In the deterministic-solve test suite, add a fixture where `runs=3` and a `solve` stub returns [fire, fire, no-fire] for a single case; assert majority=fire, stability≈0.67. Also add a `runs=2` case with [fire, no-fire] and document the tie-breaking rule (e.g., conservative=no-fire, or fire, or error).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a unit test that passes an exec-tier=AGENT/container criterion to the `run_case` criterion arm (via fake runner) and asserts it raises the expected error with a clear, non-traceback message.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new error path in the `run_case` criterion arm ('a criterion whose fixtures require [Container G3/G4 + ISF] errors with a clear message') but specifies no test for this path.
findings.location: Invocation model section: 'Container G3/G4 + ISF need a graph/log, not inline text — OUT of scope here; a criterion whose fixtures require them errors with a clear message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the tie-breaking rule for even-N majority votes (e.g. treat tie as 'fire' for conservative false-accept measurement, or flag as 'indeterminate' and exclude from recall/false_accept denominators) and document it in the metric definitions.
findings.finding: The N-run majority fire/no-fire vote is undefined when N is even and runs are split evenly (e.g. N=2, 1 fire + 1 no-fire), producing an indeterminate observed verdict rather than a deterministic calibration label.
findings.location: Invocation model & implementation contract — N-run stability

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC2 and the calibration metrics section: either define what the CLI prints for discrimination cases (e.g. a separate 'discrimination' section showing validity/impact/novelty results), or remove 'discrimination fixtures' from AC2 and restrict it to must-fire/must-not-fire only.
findings.finding: AC2 promises that `rebar criteria eval <id>` runs 'must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures' but the calibrate_criterion spec says discrimination-axis cases (validity/impact/novelty) are 'skipped, not miscounted' with no defined output for them in the calibration view. There is no protocol specifying whether discrimination cases appear in the CLI output, are counted in a total, or are silently omitted — making 'runs discrimination fixtures' an empty promise with no observable artifact.
findings.location: AC2 ('must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures'); 'Calibration metrics' section ('Only fire/no-fire cases feed the calibration... validity/impact/novelty axis cases are skipped')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the calibrate_criterion spec: 'Discrimination cases (validity/impact/novelty axis) are run and their per-case results printed, but excluded from recall/false_accept/kappa (which are fire/no-fire only). A separate discrimination summary shows the fraction of high_* cases graded ≥ 0.5 and low_* cases < 0.5.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what `rebar criteria eval` outputs for discrimination fixtures: either (a) state they are run but silently excluded from the calibration table (and update AC2 to match), or (b) define a separate display row/section for their results alongside the fire/no-fire calibration view.
findings.finding: Discrimination fixtures are listed as 'run' in AC2 and the 'What' section, but the Calibration metrics section only defines outputs for fire/no-fire cases and explicitly skips discrimination cases — leaving the CLI output for discrimination fixture results entirely unspecified. An implementer would stop to ask what (if anything) the calibration view should display for them.
findings.location: What section ('discrimination fixtures'); AC2 ('runs must-fire/must-not-fire + discrimination fixtures live'); Calibration metrics section ('validity/impact/novelty axis cases are skipped, not miscounted')
findings.suggested_fix: If discrimination fixtures are truly out of scope for this story's calibration view, update AC2 to read 'runs must-fire/must-not-fire fixtures live…' and add a note 'discrimination fixtures are loaded but not yet reported (deferred)'. If they should be reported, add a 'discrimination score' row to the calibration view spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and mitigate the new `eval_solver` → `passes.pass1_chunk` cross-layer dependency — define a thin adapter or protocol (e.g., a `CriterionRunner` callable) so the eval harness is insulated from passes-layer signature changes.
findings.finding: The criterion arm introduces a new cross-layer dependency from `eval_solver.py` (eval/solver layer) directly into `passes.pass1_chunk` (plan-review passes layer), but this coupling is not acknowledged, justified, or mitigated with an interface or event boundary. Changes to `pass1_chunk`'s signature, behavior, or availability will silently break the eval runner.
findings.location: Invocation model section: 'The arm: … passes.pass1_chunk(runner, cfg, plan=case["input"], chunk=[desc], agentic=…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a `CriterionRunner` protocol (e.g., `Callable[[runner, cfg, plan, chunk, agentic], list[Finding]]`) and inject it into `run_case`, defaulting to `passes.pass1_chunk`. This keeps the eval harness decoupled and testable without importing the passes layer directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the architectural decision to split `run_case` into agentic-store and criterion-pass1 arms in an ADR or CLAUDE.md update, explaining why `case_store` is bypassed for criterion evaluations.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — the criterion arm of `run_case` bypasses the existing agentic/store-based invocation model entirely and calls `pass1_chunk` directly — but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is mentioned to capture this decision for future maintainers.
findings.location: Invocation model section: 'The existing run_case branches … are the AGENTIC store-reader ops — structurally inapplicable to a plan-review criterion … The criterion arm therefore does NOT use case_store.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the behavior when LLM calls fail or time out during `rebar criteria eval` — define whether partial N-run results are accepted (with a warning), the run errors out, or individual failed runs are retried, and add a test or documented error message for this state.
findings.finding: The CLI `rebar criteria eval <id>` makes live LLM calls (potentially N times per fixture) but the plan does not specify behavior on LLM timeout, runner error, or partial N-run failure (e.g., 2 of 5 runs succeed before a network error).
findings.location: CLI error states + sequencing section; N-run stability section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence justification (or an explicit 'these cannot be split because…') explaining why the runner-extension and the calibration-view are kept in a single ticket rather than two sequenced stories, to satisfy the decomposition-judgment criterion.
findings.finding: The plan touches 4 files across 3 architectural layers (solver, metrics/scoring, CLI) and introduces two new public interfaces (`calibrate_criterion` + a new `run_case` arm), yet provides no rationale for why the calibration-metrics concern and the runner-extension concern are kept in a single ticket rather than sequenced as separate stories — the rubric's 'any of these signals pushes toward decompose' threshold is met on three counts.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Files: eval_solver.py, eval.py / eval_scorers.py, _llm_commands.py'; AC set (6 criteria total)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for extending `run_case` vs. adding a standalone `run_criterion_case` function (e.g. 'unified dispatch so `calibrate_criterion` calls one entry point regardless of whether the criterion is built-in or project-owned').
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for extending the existing `run_case` function with a criterion arm rather than adding a standalone `run_criterion_case` function, which would have lower blast radius for existing callers and cleaner separation of concerns.
findings.location: Invocation model & implementation contract — Criterion arm of `run_case`; Scope — Files

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
